When DIR. Matthews said suffrage
agitators were unique female speci.
mems suffering from male-itis, wom-
en- OF Seattle, strong suffrage
center, wanted to know what he
meant


What is male-itis?' So he was
asked. The construction of your
new word doesn't follow the rule
of tits word formations. tits
means inflammation appendicitis
is inflammation of the appendix.


Substitute desire for appendix,
answered DR.. Matthews. These
women are suffering from desire
tits, Inflammation of the desire to
be men or as men. They're tired
of what they call the limitations
of women. They want the freedom,
the rights, the privileges of men.
They think the ballot will give it
to them.


THE ballot is not a right; it is
a privilege conferred. 1t has al
ways been restricted and always
will be. 1t is not an inalienable
right"


Yes, but the suffrage privilege
carries with it certain rights, and
is it these rights that women
want" the interviewer suggested.


But women are not willing to
assume all the rights the ballot i'm
poses. They want to be a privi-
legged class. They can't go to war,
they can't serve on a jury-what
would a jury look like, half men
and half women? They would never
agree on a verdict. And how about
working on the street? Women
can't do that"


"All men who vote don't go to
war, serve on juries or work on
the Streets" DR.. Matthews was re-
minded.
